item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good fucking god. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: lost it at "and standup comedy" +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Florida man became a Keter level SCP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm curious what other articles you're using as context

The other articles on the wiki that I have read.

because one of the big things about SCP is "no canon"

Well, another thing is "People can form their own head canons of the Foundation". This article does not mesh with the head canon of the Foundation that I have formed. It seems to not fit in with the others. It is by that metric that it seems wrong.

So… are you talking about other arbitrary articles on the site?

Yes.

but this comes off as arbitrating "what should and should not be on the site" based on… what I can only assume is an arbitrarily constructed definition that fits your personal tastes.

Well, I am not saying it does not belong on the site. The votes indicate that it does. I am saying that it does not seem to fit in with the others. I like consistency and this article is not consistent with the others. That is not the sole reason I dislike it. That would be downvoting for non-content related reasons, I believe. My main reasons are that I don't like the "real life person is an anomaly" and I don't like how it portrays said real life person as a god.
Basically I do not like the concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm perfectly fine with mainlist articles being comedic. It's a perfectly valid genre. But this article just does not fit my sense of humour. There wasn't really anything that appealed to me in this.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm curious what other articles you're using as context… because one of the big things about SCP is "no canon", so unless you're saying this feels wrong given the context of the tale series that follows (which I have a hard time believing since this all feels very much in the same spirit). So… are you talking about other arbitrary articles on the site?

Like, your tastes are your own, but this comes off as arbitrating "what should and should not be on the site" based on… what I can only assume is an arbitrarily constructed definition that fits your personal tastes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't like this. I don't like the "real life living person is an anomaly" trope nor do I like how this is basically just him as a "god". This article feels rather wrong to me given the context of the other articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What did Florida ever do to you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I've acknowledged the latter point elsewhere in this thread, I don't see why "obscure and forbidden acting techniques" would be a break of clinical tone. It's a plain, even bare description of what they are. It's a ridiculous idea, but the Foundation is addressing it seriously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While not every funny SCP needs to be a -J, this one definitely breaks clinical tone with lines such as "obscure and forbidden acting techniques" and "flight, spontaneous materialization and dematerialization, fine manipulation of objects and substances, and stand-up comedy." Phrases like these really break my immersion, and don't feel fitting in a mainlist SCP article.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was pretty funny, and I actually like Tim Allen. Dude is fucking Buzz Lightyear. How can you hate that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This gives me a giggle fit every time I read it. Take this +1 already, you degenerate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He ascended during the filming of The Shaggy Dog, which released in early 2006. So it was in fact 7 years after his ascension.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved it because of the ending, and the fact that I grew up watching Seinfeld with my parents

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So I just noticed that on the pic it says its 7. Years after his ascension, but if his ascension was in 2006 and the pic was in 2012, that would make it 6 years

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get it now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All the little nonsensical details make this work wonderfully.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
obscure and forbidden acting techniques

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my fucking God, yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personally, I see XK Tim Duncan being so immensely different in tone, purpose, story, presentation, etc. that trying to compare the two would just be silly. They're not similar in any matter of substance.

I will admit that if the "Anomalous Celebrities Named Tim" genre gets a third entry, I will find it slightly weird.

Also, it is goofiness for goofiness' sake. It's a non-serious article meant to make you laugh, much like my SCP-3900.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this is funnier for me cause i had very little exposure to tim allen growing up.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I knew that line would be a little too far for some people. It does have in-universe basis, as the implication is that Tim Allen is literally using his powers to make himself funnier. But I agree that the presentation has been bent a bit for comedic purposes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is that image hideously blurry for anyone else?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a lot of fun, but there are a few decisions and turns of phrases that take it a step too far for me (as a non-J skip). As just one example:

SCP-4844 is capable of numerous feats including flight, spontaneous materialization and dematerialization, fine manipulation of objects and substances, and stand-up comedy.

This is the textual equivalent of the author directly 'winking' at the reader.

I recognize that, in a sense, this is very nitpicky — however, for me, part of the 'game' of SCPs is maintaining the illusion of verisimilitude. That's the challenge; finding clever ways to work around this limitation. The article has to play it straight — even when the anomaly itself is clearly ridiculous.

This is kind of a personal preference, though. YMMV. Besides, it seems to be doing fairly well regardless — so hey!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who's hand is that on his shoulder?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To me this is just goofiness for goofiness' sake, and the 'godlike celebrity' concept inevitably calls up unflattering comparisons with Massively Anomalous Tim Duncan, even though they're obviously going for very different things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted at 'Florida is absent'. It fucking deserves to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not everything funny needs to be a -J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This would be much less effective as a -J

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was about to do that but I thought it would be too obvious, I'm surprised only one person did it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great but PLEASE make this a -J

item.title: Re: Licensing Captain Post
item.content:encoded: Alright, cool! I was just worried that it being 2.0 instead of 3.0 would get it deleted.

item.title: Licensing Captain Post
item.content:encoded: 2.0 is forward compatible, and thus so is the image.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
I'm not the author, but the source is here

It's a Wikimedia Commons File, but it's under the CC BY-SA 2.0 license. I hate to say it, but I don't think the image will be able to stay, then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: aeuhhh?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He's just too powerful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hate everything about this skip, including the fact that I liked it enough to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When discussing themes, I privately joked that a skip on The Shaggy Dog would be an acceptable interpretation.

And then it fucking happened. I am both so very proud and so very disappointed.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: [Image is good]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jerry: What's the deal with Tim Allen?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: like I said in chat, i fuckin love this and it's perfect.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I hate Tim Allen.

This is a trilogy. All three parts are entries in the 144 Hour Jam Contest. God have mercy on my soul.

1. SCP-4844 - Last Man Standing (you are here)
2. What's The Deal?
3. Clash of the Cosmic Comedians

EDIT: It has officially tied for 2nd Place for Day 1 of the contest! Thank you everyone who read it, liked it, or disliked it.

Show Commentary
Hide Commentary

I was originally not planning on participating in JamCon at all, but as soon as I saw "Shaggy Dog" as a theme, I immediately thought of the movie with Tim Allen. And then, out of nowhere, the concept of this article hit me, and I wrote the first draft in about an hour.

Thanks to everyone who read it before posting, and special thanks to A Random Day who helpfully pointed out a few places to streamline.

Additional thanks to my best friend, who logged in to my account in order to post this article at the precise moment that posting opened, so that I could go to a party instead. Not only did he succeed, he managed to post before literally anyone else, so this article is the first entry in the entire contest. That achievement is entirely his.

This article was in part inspired by, for some god-forsaken reason, the entirely non-comedic comic Irredeemable, which also features an incredibly petty antagonist with godlike abilities. There's a small reference or two sprinkled throughout the trilogy.
